tools.description: Close a GitHub issue with a closing comment. Use this when work is complete, the issue is no longer relevant, or it's a duplicate. The closing comment should explain the resolution or reason for closing. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be closed. Target: *.

body.description: Closing comment explaining why the issue is being closed and summarizing any resolution, workaround, or conclusion.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to close. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 901 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/901). If omitted, closes the issue that triggered this workflow (requires an issue event trigger).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

state_reason.description: The reason for closing the issue. Use 'COMPLETED' for resolved issues, 'NOT_PLANNED' for issues that won't be addressed, or 'DUPLICATE' for duplicate issues. Defaults to 'COMPLETED'.

tools.name: close_issue

tools.description: Add a comment to an existing GitHub issue, pull request, or discussion. Use this to provide feedback, answer questions, or add information to an existing conversation. For creating new items, use create_issue, create_discussion, or create_pull_request instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 2 comment(s) can be added. Target: *. Supports reply_to_id for discussion threading.

body.description: The comment text in Markdown format. This is the 'body' field - do not use 'comment_body' or other variations. Provide helpful, relevant information that adds value to the conversation.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: The issue, pull request, or discussion number to comment on. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 123 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/123). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run. If omitted, the tool will attempt to resolve the target from the current workflow context (triggering issue, PR, or discussion). If auto-targeting fails (e.g., for schedule or workflow_dispatch triggers), the tool call will fail with an error.

reply_to_id.description: Node ID of the discussion comment to reply to, enabling threaded discussion comments. When provided, the new comment is posted as a reply to the specified top-level discussion comment. If the given node ID belongs to a nested reply, the handler automatically resolves it to the top-level parent. Only applicable for discussion comments — ignored for issue and pull request comments.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

temporary_id.description: Unique temporary identifier for this comment. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Auto-generated if not provided. The temporary ID is returned in the tool response so you can reference this comment later.

tools.name: add_comment

tools.description: Add labels to an existing GitHub issue or pull request for categorization and filtering. Labels must already exist in the repository. For creating new issues with labels, use create_issue with the labels property instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be added. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to add labels to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, adds labels to the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to add (e.g., ['bug', 'priority-high']). Labels must exist in the repository. This field is required — omitting it will cause a validation error.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: add_labels

tools.description: Remove labels from an existing GitHub issue or pull request. If a label is not present on the item, it will be silently skipped. Use this to clean up labels that are no longer applicable. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 7 label(s) can be removed. Target: *.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

item_number.description: Issue or PR number to remove labels from. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 456 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/456). If omitted, removes labels from the item that triggered this workflow.

labels.description: Label names to remove (e.g., ['bug', 'needs-triage']). Labels that don't exist on the item are silently skipped. At least one label must be provided.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: remove_labels

tools.description: Assign one or more GitHub users to an issue. Use this to delegate work to specific team members. Users must have access to the repository.

assignee.description: Single GitHub username to assign. Use 'assignees' array for multiple users.

assignees.description: GitHub usernames to assign to the issue (e.g., ['octocat', 'mona']). Users must have access to the repository.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

issue_number.description: Issue number to assign users to. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 543 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/543). If omitted, assigns to the issue that triggered this workflow.

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: assign_to_user

tools.description: Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tool.description: Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.

tools.name: missing_tool

tools.description: Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

message.description: Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: noop

tools.description: Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.

alternatives.description: Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).

context.description: Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).

data_type.description: Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.

integrity.description: Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., "low", "medium", "high").

reason.description: Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).

secrecy.description: Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., "public", "internal", "private").

tools.name: missing_data

tools.description: Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.

details.description: Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).

reason.description: A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.

tools.name: report_incomplete

tools.description: Post a routing comment @mentioning team owners on the triggering issue; bypasses safe-outputs mention neutralization

message.description: The comment body text without any @mentions or @ symbols

owners.description: Comma-separated GitHub usernames to notify, without the @ prefix (e.g. 'user1, user2, Azure/team-name')

tools.name: mention_owners

annotations.title: Get a specific label from a repository.

tools.description: Get a specific label from a repository.
tools.name: get_label

tools.description: Get information about a specific issue in a GitHub repository.

method.description:
The read operation to perform on a single issue.
Options are:
1. get - Get details of a specific issue.
2. get_comments - Get issue comments.
3. get_sub_issues - Get sub-issues of the issue.
4. get_labels - Get labels assigned to the issue.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

tools.name: issue_read

tools.description: List supported issue types for repository owner (organization).

owner.description: The organization owner of the repository

tools.name: list_issue_types

tools.description: List issues in a GitHub repository. For pagination, use the 'endCursor' from the previous response's 'pageInfo' in the 'after' parameter.

after.description: Cursor for pagination. Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.

direction.description: Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.

orderBy.description: Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

state.description: Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided

tools.name: list_issues

tools.description: Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue

owner.description: Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.

perPage.description: Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)

query.description: Search query using GitHub issues search syntax

repo.description: Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.

sort.description: Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match

tools.name: search_issues

content.text: {"number":58410,"title":"[Core] Docs: Azure.Core 1.54.0-beta.1 CHANGELOG missing AppSettings schema bug fix entry","body":"## Documentation Gap **Package:** `Azure.Core` **Service directory:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/` **Triggered by:** PR #58408 by `@m-nash` ## What Changed PR #58408 fixed a bug in `sdk/core/Azure.Core/schema/ConfigurationSchema.json` where the top-level `definitions/credential` declared `required: ["CredentialSource"]` — the same property already required by `System.ClientModel`'s schema segment. When the appsettings schema combiner merges segments by definition name, it concatenates `required` arrays without deduplication, producing `["CredentialSource", "CredentialSource"]` in the consumer's combined `AppSettingsSchema.json`. The fix removes the duplicate declaration from Azure.Core's schema segment. ## Gaps Found - The `### Bugs Fixed` section under `## 1.54.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` in `sdk/core/Azure.Core/CHANGELOG.md` is empty — there is no entry documenting this fix. - The `README.md` does not describe the AppSettings / `appsettings.json` IntelliSense schema feature at all (it was introduced in 1.52.0), so users encountering the duplicate-`required` issue have no documentation to reference. 📐 Implementation Guide This section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above. ### Step 1: Modify files **File:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/CHANGELOG.md` **Action:** Edit — add a `Bugs Fixed` entry under `## 1.54.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)`. Replace the empty `### Bugs Fixed` section: ```markdown ### Bugs Fixed ``` with: ```markdown ### Bugs Fixed - Fixed `ConfigurationSchema.json` credential definition to remove a duplicate top-level `required: ["CredentialSource"]` entry. When multiple schema segments are merged into `AppSettingsSchema.json`, the combiner concatenates `required` arrays without deduplication, producing `["CredentialSource", "CredentialSource"]` in the combined file. Because `System.ClientModel` already asserts this requirement, removing it from Azure.Core's segment eliminates the duplicate with no change in validation behavior. ([#58408](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/pull/58408)) ``` **File:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` **Action:** Edit — add a section under `## Key concepts` (after the existing credential bullet) explaining the AppSettings JSON schema feature. Insert the following after the line about `DefaultAzureCredential`/`ManagedIdentityCredential` and before the `### Thread safety` heading: ```markdown ### Configuring clients via `appsettings.json` (IntelliSense schema) Starting with Azure.Core 1.52.0, the NuGet package ships a `ConfigurationSchema.json` segment. When a consumer references Azure.Core (directly or transitively), the build tooling merges these segments into `obj/{config}/{tfm}/AppSettingsSchema.json`. This enables: - **IntelliSense / autocompletion** for `AzureClients` configuration blocks in `appsettings.json` inside Visual Studio and VS Code. - **Validation** of `Retry`, `Diagnostics`, and `CredentialSource` options at edit time. No additional setup is required — the schema is automatically merged during `dotnet build`. ``` ### Step 2: Add or update code snippets No new executable snippets are required for this change. The CHANGELOG entry and README prose update above are sufficient. ### Step 3: Verify README structure The README at `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` currently contains all required sections. No structural changes are needed beyond the new prose paragraph added in Step 1. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/core/Azure.Core/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/core/Azure.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 core` — verify no unintended README changes. 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 core` — no public API changes expected; verify the output is unchanged. 5. `dotnet format sdk/core/Azure.Core/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains > > The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `pkgs.dev.azure.com` > - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "pkgs.dev.azure.com" > - "releaseassets.githubusercontent.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24693941236/agentic_workflow) · ● 408.9K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58410","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-20T22:43:20Z","updated_at":"2026-04-20T22:43:20Z"}

No structural changes are needed beyond the new prose paragraph added in Step 1. ### Step 4: Validate Run these commands in order: 1. `dotnet build sdk/core/Azure.Core/` 2. `dotnet test sdk/core/Azure.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live` 3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 core` — verify no unintended README changes. 4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 core` — no public API changes expected; verify the output is unchanged. 5. `dotnet format sdk/core/Azure.Core/` ## Next Steps > [!TIP] > **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above > [!WARNING] > > ⚠️ Firewall blocked 2 domains > > The following domains were blocked by the firewall during workflow execution: > > - `pkgs.dev.azure.com` > - `releaseassets.githubusercontent.com` > > To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter: > > ```yaml > network: > allowed: > - defaults > - "pkgs.dev.azure.com" > - "releaseassets.githubusercontent.com" > ``` > > See [Network Configuration](https://github.github.com/gh-aw/reference/network/) for more information. > > > Generated by [Update Docs](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/actions/runs/24693941236/agentic_workflow) · ● 408.9K · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3AAzure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+Azure%2Fazure-sdk-for-net%2Fupdate-samples-and-docs%22&type=issues) ","state":"open","html_url":"https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/58410","user":{"login":"github-actions[bot]","id":41898282,"profile_url":"https://github.com/apps/github-actions","avatar_url":"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/in/15368?v=4"},"author_association":"NONE","reactions":{"total_count":0,"+1":0,"-1":0,"laugh":0,"confused":0,"heart":0,"hooray":0,"rocket":0,"eyes":0},"created_at":"2026-04-20T22:43:20Z","updated_at":"2026-04-20T22:43:20Z"}

{"total_count":119,"incomplete_results":false,"items":[{"id":3999485256,"number":56583,"state":"closed","state_reason":"completed","locked":false,"title":"[QUERY] Clarify relation to Microsoft.Extensions.Azure","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.Core 1.51.0

### Query/Question

Hi,

Could you please clarify how the new [configuration and dependency injection support](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/issues/55491) relates to [Microsoft.Extensions.Azure](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/tree/main/sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure)'s support for configuration and dependency injection through its [ClientFactory](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/[REDACTED]/sdk/extensions/Microsoft.Extensions.Azure/src/Internal/ClientFactory.cs#L95-L340). Reading through [Azure.Core's documentation](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/sdk/core/System.ClientModel/src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md) on the subject, Microsoft.Extensions.Azure and Azure.Core seem to solve the same problem in incompatible ways, but I might be missing something here.

BR,
Per

### Environment

System.ClientModel 1.9.0 dependencies","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.Core 1.51.1 -> System.ClientModel 1.9.0

### Query/Question

I might not understand this correctly, so could someone clarify?  
Azure.Core 1.51.1 has a dependency to System.ClientModel 1.9.0

"Configuration and Dependency Injection" link on latest `Azure.Identity` release (1.18.0) is broken","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.Identity 1.18.0

### Query/Question

Just received the release notification for [`Azure.Identity 1.18.0`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/releases/tag/Azure.Identity_1.18.0) and immediately saw this section:

> Added experimental Microsoft.Extensions.Configuration and Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection integration for Azure SDK clients. For details, see the [Configuration and Dependency Injection](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/release/Azure.Identity_1.18.0/sdk/core/Azure.Core/src/docs/ConfigurationAndDependencyInjection.md) documentation.

But this link is broken.

I'm extremely interested in what this new capability is so would really appreciate if someone could fix or point to the correct document.

### Environment

When upgrading from Azure.Core 1.46.1 to 1.47.1 System.Memory.Data fails to load on RaspBerryPi (Rasbian Linux-Arm)","body":"### Library name and version

Azure.Core 1.47.1

### Describe the bug

When upgrading from Azure.Core 1.46.1 to 1.47.1 System.Memory.Data fails to load on RaspBerryPi (Raspbian Linux-Arm) when trying to create an azure table

Sample Code:
```c#
var tableClient = new TableServiceClient(connectionString);
table = tableClient.GetTableClient("table");
 await table.CreateIfNotExistsAsync();
```

Exception
_Could not load file or assembly 'System.Memory.Data, Version=8.0.0.1, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=[REDACTED] The system cannot find the file specified._


<img width="1208" height="449" alt="Image" src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7a55a8e7-d969-4a47-9efd-ac83bd8d1c41" />


**_**Note** when forcing/including a newer version of the System.Memory.Data, like version 9.0.8 package the issue goes away._**


### Expected behavior

The code works and creates a table if not exist

### Actual behavior

Exception is thrown 

### Reproduction Steps

Run the code below on a raspberry pi or linux arm using Azure.Core 1.47.1 and System.Memory.Data 8.0.1

Sample Code:
```c#
var tableClient = new TableServiceClient(connectionString);
table = tableClient.GetTableClient("table");
 await table.CreateIfNotExistsAsync();
```


### Environment

RaspberryPi 2 with Raspbian
PRETTY_NAME="Raspbian GNU/Linux 12 (bookworm)"
NAME="Raspbian GNU/Linux"
VERSION_ID="12"
.NET 9 
Azure.Core 1.47.1
System.Memory.Data 8.0.1

arguments.body: Thank you for your feedback. Tagging and routing to the team member(s) best able to assist.

## 🎯 Agentic Issue Triage

**Summary:** This bot-filed issue identifies documentation gaps in `Azure.Core`: the `CHANGELOG.md` for 1.54.0-beta.1 is missing a `Bugs Fixed` entry for the `ConfigurationSchema.json` duplicate-`required` fix from PR #58408, and `README.md` lacks documentation for the AppSettings JSON IntelliSense schema feature introduced in 1.52.0.

<details>
<summary>📋 Issue Details</summary>

**Package:** `Azure.Core` (unreleased 1.54.0-beta.1)
**Affected API:** `sdk/core/Azure.Core/schema/ConfigurationSchema.json`, `CHANGELOG.md`, `README.md`
**Scenarios:**
- `CHANGELOG.md` `### Bugs Fixed` section under `## 1.54.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` is empty — missing entry for the duplicate `required: ["CredentialSource"]` fix
- `README.md` has no documentation for the AppSettings / `appsettings.json` IntelliSense schema feature (introduced in 1.52.0)

**Root ask:** Add a CHANGELOG entry documenting the schema fix and add a README section explaining the AppSettings JSON schema feature.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🔎 Debugging / Reproduction Notes</summary>

The bug was fixed by PR #58408: `ConfigurationSchema.json` had a duplicate top-level `required: ["CredentialSource"]` which, when merged by the appsettings schema combiner, produced `["CredentialSource", "CredentialSource"]` in the combined `AppSettingsSchema.json`. Since `System.ClientModel` already declares this requirement, the duplicate was safe to remove.

**Suggested investigation steps:**
1. Verify `sdk/core/Azure.Core/CHANGELOG.md` under `## 1.54.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` — confirm `### Bugs Fixed` is empty.
2. Add the CHANGELOG entry referencing PR #58408 as described in the issue.
3. Check `sdk/core/Azure.Core/README.md` under `## Key concepts` — confirm no AppSettings schema section exists.
4. Add the README prose section describing the IntelliSense schema feature.
</details>

<details>
<summary>🏷️ Label Confidence</summary>

- **Category:** `Client` — Azure.Core is a core client library, not management or provisioning
- **Service:** `Azure.Core` — explicitly named in the issue title and body (`sdk/core/Azure.Core/`)
- **Confidence:** High — package and service directory are explicitly stated; reference issue #56583 (`Client` + `Azure.Core`) confirms the label pattern
</details>

<details>
<summary>👥 Owner Routing</summary>

- **Matched CODEOWNERS entry:** `# ServiceLabel: %Azure.Core` (line 60) — only ServiceLabel entry for Azure.Core in the file
- **AzureSdkOwners:** `@m-nash`
- **ServiceOwners:** none listed
- **Routing action:** Assigned `@m-nash`; no `needs-team-attention` added (issue is bot-filed, not `customer-reported`)
- **Scan notes:** Scanned from bottom of CODEOWNERS upward; no more-specific multi-label entry for `%Azure.Core` exists. The `%Azure.Core` entry at line 60 was the first (and only) match.
</details>
